approach: GP with UCB/EI acquisition. Week 1 submissions were generated before initial .npy data was integrated into the surrogate — settings not recorded in meta (pre-tracking).

1.note: Top-right corner. Kernel later changed to matern — RBF inappropriate for localised hotspot.

2.note: Low-X1, high-X2 — moved away from known initial best at [0.703, 0.927]. Over-exploration.

3.note: High-A, low-B region. Initial best has high-B (~0.61) — this confirmed B matters.

4.note: Extreme values (P2≈0, P3≈1). Exploratory but too far from best region.

5.note: High-C1, low-C2 — opposite of best initial [0.224, 0.846, 0.879, 0.879]. Over-exploration.

6.note: High Sugar and Milk vs initial best (low both). Result confirmed penalising effect.

7.note: Informed start heuristic. High subsample/max_features, low regularisation — opposite of initial best.

8.note: Near initial best range. Result 9.26 vs initial best 9.60 — reasonable but not improved.

week_summary: All 8 portal submissions landed below the best known value from initial data. Primary cause: initial .npy data was not yet loaded into the GP when suggestions were generated — surrogate had near-zero training data. This has been fixed for week 2 onwards. Key learning: initial data integration is essential before generating any suggestions.
